title: salix × cremnophila
intro: Salix × cremnophila, also known as rock willow, is a rare hybrid species of willow tree, resulting from the cross between Salix scouleriana and Salix lucida. The bark of this plant contains salicin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which makes it useful for medicinal purposes. The health benefits of Salix × cremnophila include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, and it has also been traditionally used to treat fever, headaches, and rheumatism. Salix × cremnophila is often prepared as a tincture or infusion, and it can also be made into a salve or cream for topical use.
